title: audit — PHP attribute contracts
description: This module ships ZERO owned PHP attributes. Capture is exclusively via the vendor `owen-it/laravel-auditing` package's `Auditable` trait — there is no `AsAuditEventCapturer`, no `AsAuditProviderDriver`, no `AsAuditableTransition` attribute. Modules opt in to auditing by composing the vendor trait on their Eloquent models. Custom audit events register via the vendor's `Auditable::registerAuditableTransition` seam at boot — NOT via a PHP attribute discovery pass.
note: Contrast with the growth::analytics + growth::marketing modules, which DO ship attribute-driven capturer discovery (#[AsAnalyticsEventCapturer], #[AsMarketingProviderDriver]). Those modules translate DOMAIN events into their own fan-out signals via a compilation pass. Audit does NOT — the vendor observer captures every model mutation directly, without an intermediate mapping layer.

vendor_extension_seam.when: Boot time, once per registered custom event
consumers_that_will_register_customs: auth::UserLoggedInAuditableTransition (Wave 6) — captures 'auth.login' events as auditable transitions on the User model

finance::TransactionApprovedAuditableTransition (Wave 6+) — captures 'transaction.approved' events on Transaction rows without a direct field mutation (transitions from pending to approved state via a state machine, not a column update)
vendor_extension_seam.discovery_pattern: Consumer modules add their AuditableTransition registrations in their module's ServiceProvider::boot() method. This module's AuditServiceProvider does not scan for them (unlike growth::analytics which does a compilation pass) — the vendor seam handles registration on its own.
vendor_extension_seam.audited_events_catalog: The union of vendor default events + registered custom transitions lives in data/audited-events-catalog.json (regenerated at boot). AuditObserver::creating validates the `event` column against this closed set.
